contents: From HoTT Require Import Basics Types HSpace.Core HSpace.Coherent HSpace.Pointwise

From HoTT Require Import Basics Types HSpace.Core HSpace.Coherent HSpace.Pointwise
  Pointed Homotopy.EvaluationFibration.

(** * The moduli type of coherent H-space structures *)

(** When [A] is a left-invertible coherent H-space, we construct an equivalence between the ("moduli") type of coherent H-space structures on [A] and the type [A ->* (A ->** A)]. By the smash-hom adjunction for pointed types, due to Floris van Doorn in HoTT, the latter is also equivalent to the type [Smash A A ->* A].

This equivalence generalizes a formula of Arkowitz--Curjel and Copeland for spaces, and appears as Theorem 2.27 in https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.02636v1 *)

(** ** Paths between H-space structures *)

(** Paths between H-space structures correspond to homotopies between the underlying binary operations which respect the identities. This is the type of the latter. *)
Definition path_ishspace_type {X : pType} (mu nu : IsHSpace X) : Type.
destruct mu as [mu mu_lid mu_rid], nu as [nu nu_lid nu_rid].
refine { h : forall x0 x1, mu x0 x1 = nu x0 x1 & prod (forall x:X, _) (forall x:X, _) }.

hypotheses.type: forall x0 x1 : X, mu x0 x1 = nu x0 x1
forall x0 x1 : X, mu x0 x1 = nu x0 x1
forall x0 x1 : X, mu x0 x1 = nu x0 x1

contents: exact (mu_lid x = h pt x @ nu_lid x).

hypotheses.type: forall x0 x1 : X, mu x0 x1 = nu x0 x1

contents: exact (mu_rid x = h x pt @ nu_rid x).
(** Transport of left and right identities of binary operations along paths between the underlying functions. *)

Local Definition transport_binop_lr_id `{Funext} {X : Type} {x : X}
  {mu nu : X -> X -> X} `{mu_lid : forall y, mu x y = y}
  `{mu_rid : forall y, mu y x = y} (p : mu = nu)
  : transport (fun m : X -> X -> X =>
                 (forall y, m x y = y) * (forall y, m y x = y))
      p (mu_lid, mu_rid)
    = (fun y => (ap100 p _ _)^ @ mu_lid y,
         fun y => (ap100 p _ _)^ @ mu_rid y).

goals.conclusion:
transport
  (fun m : X -> X -> X =>
   (forall y : X, m x y = y) * (forall y : X, m y x = y))
  p (mu_lid, mu_rid) =
(fun y : X => (ap100 p x y)^ @ mu_lid y,
fun y : X => (ap100 p y x)^ @ mu_rid y)

hypotheses.type: forall y : X, mu x y = y
forall y : X, mu y x = y

goals.conclusion:
transport
  (fun m : X -> X -> X =>
   (forall y : X, m x y = y) * (forall y : X, m y x = y))
  p (mu_lid, mu_rid) =
(fun y : X => (ap100 p x y)^ @ mu_lid y,
fun y : X => (ap100 p y x)^ @ mu_rid y)

hypotheses.type: forall y : X, mu x y = y
forall y : X, mu y x = y

goals.conclusion: (mu_lid, mu_rid) = (fun y : X => 1 @ mu_lid y, fun y : X => 1 @ mu_rid y)

hypotheses.type: forall y : X, mu x y = y
forall y : X, mu y x = y
forall y0 : X, mu x y0 = y0
forall y0 : X, mu y0 x = y0
forall y0 : X, mu x y0 = y0
forall y0 : X, mu y0 x = y0

contents: (** Characterization of paths between H-space structures. *)

Definition equiv_path_ishspace `{Funext} {X : pType} (mu nu : IsHSpace X)
  : path_ishspace_type mu nu <~> (mu = nu).

destruct mu as [mu mu_lid mu_rid], nu as [nu nu_lid nu_rid];
    unfold path_ishspace_type.

goals.conclusion:
{h : forall x0 x1 : X, mu x0 x1 = nu x0 x1 &
((forall x : X, mu_lid x = h pt x @ nu_lid x) *
 (forall x : X, mu_rid x = h x pt @ nu_rid x))%type} <~>
{p : mu = nu &
transport
  (fun mu0 : X -> X -> X =>
   ((forall x : X, mu0 pt x = x) * (forall x : X, mu0 x pt = x))%type)
  p (mu_lid, mu_rid) =
(nu_lid, nu_rid)}

(forall x : X, mu_lid x = h pt x @ nu_lid x) *
(forall x : X, mu_rid x = h x pt @ nu_rid x) <~>
transport
  (fun mu0 : X -> X -> X =>
   ((forall x : X, mu0 pt x = x) * (forall x : X, mu0 x pt = x))%type)
  (path_forall mu nu
     (functor_forall idmap (fun a : X => path_forall (mu a) (nu a)) h))
  (mu_lid, mu_rid) =
(nu_lid, nu_rid)

hypotheses.type: forall x y : X, mu x y = nu x y

goals.conclusion:
transport
  (fun mu0 : X -> X -> X =>
   ((forall x : X, mu0 pt x = x) * (forall x : X, mu0 x pt = x))%type)
  (path_forall mu nu
     (functor_forall idmap (fun a : X => path_forall (mu a) (nu a)) h))
  (mu_lid, mu_rid) =
?Goal

hypotheses.type: forall x y : X, mu x y = nu x y

goals.conclusion:
(forall x : X, mu_lid x = h pt x @ nu_lid x) *
(forall x : X, mu_rid x = h x pt @ nu_rid x) <~> ?Goal = (nu_lid, nu_rid)

hypotheses.type: forall x y : X, mu x y = nu x y

goals.conclusion:
(forall x : X, mu_lid x = h pt x @ nu_lid x) *
(forall x : X, mu_rid x = h x pt @ nu_rid x) <~>
(fun y : X =>
 (ap100
    (path_forall mu nu
       (functor_forall idmap (fun a : X => path_forall (mu a) (nu a)) h))
    pt y)^ @
 mu_lid y,
fun y : X =>
(ap100
   (path_forall mu nu
      (functor_forall idmap (fun a : X => path_forall (mu a) (nu a)) h))
   y pt)^ @
mu_rid y) = (nu_lid, nu_rid)

hypotheses.type: forall x y : X, mu x y = nu x y

goals.conclusion:
(forall x : X, mu_lid x = h pt x @ nu_lid x) *
(forall x : X, mu_rid x = h x pt @ nu_rid x) <~>
((fun y : X =>
  (ap100
     (path_forall mu nu
        (functor_forall idmap (fun a : X => path_forall (mu a) (nu a)) h))
     pt y)^ @
  mu_lid y) =
 nu_lid) *
((fun y : X =>
  (ap100
     (path_forall mu nu
        (functor_forall idmap (fun a : X => path_forall (mu a) (nu a)) h))
     y pt)^ @
  mu_rid y) =
 nu_rid)

hypotheses.type: forall x y : X, mu x y = nu x y

goals.conclusion:
mu_lid x = h pt x @ nu_lid x <~>
(ap100
   (path_forall mu nu
      (functor_forall idmap (fun a : X => path_forall (mu a) (nu a)) h))
   pt x)^ @
mu_lid x = nu_lid x

hypotheses.type: forall x0 y : X, mu x0 y = nu x0 y

goals.conclusion:
mu_rid x = h x pt @ nu_rid x <~>
(ap100
   (path_forall mu nu
      (functor_forall idmap (fun a : X => path_forall (mu a) (nu a)) h))
   x pt)^ @
mu_rid x = nu_rid x

hypotheses.type: forall x0 y : X, mu x0 y = nu x0 y

contents:
(** ** Sections of evaluation fibrations *)

(** We first show that coherent H-space structures on a pointed type correspond to pointed sections of the evaluation fibration [ev A]. *)

Definition equiv_iscohhspace_psect `{Funext} (A : pType)
  : IsCohHSpace A <~> pSect (ev A).

goals.conclusion:
{s : A -> selfmaps A &
{p : s pt = pt &
{H0 : (fun x : A => ev A (s x)) == idmap &
H0 pt = ap (ev A) p @ point_eq (ev A)}}} <~>
{hspace_op : SgOp A &
{hspace_left_identity : LeftIdentity hspace_op pt &
{hspace_right_identity : RightIdentity hspace_op pt &
hspace_left_identity pt = hspace_right_identity pt}}}

{s : A -> selfmaps A &
{p : s pt = pt &
{H0 : (fun x : A => ev A (s x)) == idmap &
H0 pt = ap (ev A) p @ point_eq (ev A)}}} <~>
{hspace_op : A -> A -> A &
{hspace_left_identity : forall y : A, hspace_op pt y = y &
{hspace_right_identity : forall x : A, hspace_op x pt = x &
hspace_left_identity pt = hspace_right_identity pt}}}

{p : mu pt = pt &
{H0 : (fun x : A => ev A (mu x)) == idmap &
H0 pt = ap (ev A) p @ point_eq (ev A)}} <~>
{hspace_left_identity : forall y : A, mu pt y = y &
{hspace_right_identity : forall x : A, mu x pt = x &
hspace_left_identity pt = hspace_right_identity pt}}

{H0 : (fun x : A => mu x pt) == idmap &
H0 pt = ap (fun f : A -> A => f pt) H1 @ 1} <~>
{hspace_right_identity : forall x : A, mu x pt = x &
apD10 H1 pt = hspace_right_identity pt}
H2 pt = ap (fun f : A -> A => f pt) H1 @ 1 <~> apD10 H1 pt = H2 pt

hypotheses.type: (fun x : A => mu x pt) == idmap

goals.conclusion: H2 pt = ap (fun f : A -> A => f pt) H1 @ 1 <~> H2 pt = apD10 H1 pt

hypotheses.type: (fun x : A => mu x pt) == idmap

goals.conclusion: ap (fun f : A -> A => f pt) H1 @ 1 = apD10 H1 pt

hypotheses.type: (fun x : A => mu x pt) == idmap

contents:
(** Our next goal is to see that when [A] is a left-invertible H-space, then the fibration [ev A] is trivial. We begin with two results that allow the domain to be a general pointed type [B]. We'll later just need the case when [B] is [A]. *)

(** This lemma says that the family [fun a => B ->* [A,a]] is trivial. *)
(** The next result is a consequence of the previous lemma. *)
transitivity {a : A  & {f : B -> A & f pt = a}}.

(** The equivalence [equiv_map_pmap_hspace] is pointed when [B] is [A]. (Note that [selfmaps A] is pointed at [idmap].) This is a pointed equivalence between the domains of [psnd : (A ->* A) * A ->* A] and [ev A : selfmaps A -> A], respectively. *)
(** When [A] is coherent, the pointed equivalence [pequiv_map_pmap_hspace] is a pointed equivalence over [A], i.e., a trivialization of [ev A]. *)

goals.conclusion: ?p pt = dpoint_eq (ev A o* pequiv_map_pmap_hspace) @ (dpoint_eq psnd)^

((fun x0 : [(A ->* A) * A, (pmap_idmap, pt)] =>
  (fun (f : A ->* A) (x : A) =>
   ap (hspace_op x) (dpoint_eq f) @ hspace_right_identity x
   :
   (ev A o* pequiv_map_pmap_hspace) (f, x) = psnd (f, x)) 
    (fst x0) (snd x0))
 :
 ev A o* pequiv_map_pmap_hspace == psnd) pt =
dpoint_eq (ev A o* pequiv_map_pmap_hspace) @ (dpoint_eq psnd)^

1 @ hspace_right_identity pt =
(ap (fun f : A -> A => f pt)
   (path_forall (fun x : A => pt * x) idmap hspace_left_identity) @
 1) @
1

(ap (fun f : A -> A => f pt)
   (path_forall (fun x : A => pt * x) idmap hspace_left_identity) @
 1) @
1 = hspace_right_identity pt

ap (fun f : A -> A => f pt)
  (path_forall (fun x : A => pt * x) idmap hspace_left_identity) =
hspace_right_identity pt

contents: (** Here is a third characterization of the type of coherent H-space structures. It simply involves shuffling the data around and using [Funext]. *)

Definition equiv_iscohhspace_ptd_action `{Funext} (A : pType)
  : IsCohHSpace A <~> { act : forall a, A ->* [A,a] & act pt ==* pmap_idmap }.

goals.conclusion:
IsCohHSpace A <~>
{act : forall a : IsPointed A, A ->* [A, a] & act pt ==* pmap_idmap}

IsCohHSpace A <~>
{act : forall a : IsPointed A, A ->* [A, a] & act pt ==* pmap_idmap}

{hspace_op : SgOp A &
{hspace_left_identity : LeftIdentity hspace_op pt &
{hspace_right_identity : RightIdentity hspace_op pt &
hspace_left_identity pt = hspace_right_identity pt}}} <~>
{act : forall a : IsPointed A, A ->* [A, a] & act pt ==* pmap_idmap}

{hspace_op : SgOp A &
{hspace_left_identity : LeftIdentity hspace_op pt &
{hspace_right_identity : RightIdentity hspace_op pt &
hspace_left_identity pt = hspace_right_identity pt}}} <~>
{act : forall a : A, A ->* [A, a] & act pt ==* pmap_idmap}

contents: (* First we shuffle the data on the LHS to be of this form: *)
equiv_via {s : {act : A -> (A -> A) & forall a, act a pt = a} & {h : s.1 pt == idmap & h pt = s.2 pt}}.

goals.conclusion:
{hspace_op : SgOp A &
{hspace_left_identity : LeftIdentity hspace_op pt &
{hspace_right_identity : RightIdentity hspace_op pt &
hspace_left_identity pt = hspace_right_identity pt}}} <~>
{s : {act : A -> A -> A & forall a : A, act a pt = a} &
{h : s.1 pt == idmap & h pt = s.2 pt}}

contents: (* Then we break up [->*] and [==*] on the RHS using issig lemmas, and handle a trailing [@ 1]. *)

goals.conclusion:
{act : A -> A -> A & forall a : A, act a pt = a} <~>
(forall a : A, A ->* [A, a])

forall a : {act : A -> A -> A & forall a : A, act a pt = a},
(fun s : {act : A -> A -> A & forall a0 : A, act a0 pt = a0} =>
 {h : s.1 pt == idmap & h pt = s.2 pt}) a <~>
(fun act : forall a0 : A, A ->* [A, a0] => act pt ==* pmap_idmap) (?f a)

{act : A -> A -> A & forall a : A, act a pt = a} <~>
(forall a : A, A ->* [A, a])

{act : A -> A -> A & forall a : A, act a pt = a} <~>
(forall a : IsPointed A, {f : A -> [A, a] & f pt = pt})

{act : A -> A -> A & forall a : A, act a pt = a} <~>
(forall a : A, {f : A -> [A, a] & f pt = pt})

forall a : {act : A -> A -> A & forall a : A, act a pt = a},
(fun s : {act : A -> A -> A & forall a0 : A, act a0 pt = a0} =>
 {h : s.1 pt == idmap & h pt = s.2 pt}) a <~>
(fun act : forall a0 : A, A ->* [A, a0] => act pt ==* pmap_idmap)
  ((equiv_functor_forall_id (fun a0 : IsPointed A => issig_pmap A [A, a0])
    oE (equiv_sig_coind (fun _ : A => A -> A)
          (fun (x : A) (f : A -> [A, x]) => f pt = pt)
        :
        {act : A -> A -> A & forall a0 : A, act a0 pt = a0} <~>
        (forall a0 : IsPointed A, {f : A -> [A, a0] & f pt = pt})))
     a)

forall a : {act : A -> A -> A & forall a : A, act a pt = a},
{h : a.1 pt == idmap & h pt = a.2 pt} <~>
{| pointed_fun := a.1 pt; dpoint_eq := a.2 pt |} ==* pmap_idmap

{h : act pt == idmap & h pt = p pt} <~>
{| pointed_fun := act pt; dpoint_eq := p pt |} ==* pmap_idmap

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, act a pt = a

goals.conclusion:
{h : act pt == idmap & h pt = p pt} <~>
{p0 : act pt == idmap & p0 pt = p pt @ 1}

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, act a pt = a

goals.conclusion: q pt = p pt <~> q pt = p pt @ 1

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, act a pt = a

contents: (** It follows that any homogeneous type is a coherent H-space.  This generalizes [ishspace_homogeneous]. *)

Definition iscohhspace_homogeneous `{Funext} {A : pType} `{IsHomogeneous A}
  : IsCohHSpace A.

goals.conclusion: {act : forall a : IsPointed A, A ->* [A, a] & act pt ==* pmap_idmap}

contents: (** One can also show directly that the H-space structure defined by [ishspace_homogeneous] is coherent. This also avoids [Funext]. *)

goals.conclusion:
eisretr (ishomogeneous pt) pt =
ap (ishomogeneous pt) (moveR_equiv_V pt pt (point_eq (ishomogeneous pt))^) @
point_eq (ishomogeneous pt)

eisretr (ishomogeneous pt) pt =
ap (ishomogeneous pt) (moveR_equiv_V pt pt (point_eq (ishomogeneous pt))^) @
point_eq (ishomogeneous pt)

contents: change (eisretr f pt = ap f (moveR_equiv_V pt pt (point_eq f)^) @ point_eq f).

goals.conclusion: eisretr f pt = ap f (moveR_equiv_V pt pt (point_eq f)^) @ point_eq f
eisretr f pt = ap f (moveR_equiv_V pt pt 1^) @ 1
eisretr f pt = ap f (1 @ eissect f pt) @ 1
eisretr f pt = ap f (1 @ eissect f pt)
ap f (eissect f pt) = ap f (1 @ eissect f pt)
eissect f pt = 1 @ eissect f pt

contents: (** Using either of these, we can "upgrade" any left-invertible H-space structure to a coherent one. This one has a prime because the direct proof below computes better. *)
(** The new multiplication is homotopic to the original one.  Relative to this, we expect that one of the identity laws also agrees, but that the other does not. *)

goals.conclusion: (fun a b : A => a * (sg_op pt)^-1 b) = hspace_op

contents: (* [*], [sg_op] and [hspace_op] all denote the original operation. *)

goals.conclusion: a * (sg_op pt)^-1 b = hspace_op a b

contents: (** Here's a different proof that directly upgrades an H-space structure, leaving the multiplication and left-identity definitionally the same, but changing the right-identity. *)

goals.conclusion:
hspace_left_identity pt =
(ap (sg_op pt) (hspace_right_identity pt))^ @
(ap (sg_op pt) (hspace_left_identity pt) @ hspace_right_identity pt)

ap (sg_op pt) (hspace_right_identity pt) @ hspace_left_identity pt =
ap (sg_op pt) (hspace_left_identity pt) @ hspace_right_identity pt

hspace_left_identity (hspace_op pt pt) @ hspace_right_identity pt =
ap (sg_op pt) (hspace_left_identity pt) @ hspace_right_identity pt

hspace_left_identity (hspace_op pt pt) =
ap (sg_op pt) (hspace_left_identity pt)

hspace_left_identity (hspace_op pt pt) @ hspace_left_identity pt =
ap (sg_op pt) (hspace_left_identity pt) @ hspace_left_identity pt
